skill.name: design-style-skill
skill.description: Helps choose consistent PPT visual styles using radius, spacing, typography, and component rules for four slide design systems.
skill.summary: Apply consistent radius and spacing recipes to PPT slide designs.
skill.author: MiniMax-AI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported Ruby shell backtick execution and weak cryptographic algorithms. Review found these are false positives in Markdown front matter and JavaScript documentation examples, with no executable scripts, network calls, credential access, or prompt injection text.

low_findings.title: Static False Positive: Markdown Code Fence Backticks
low_findings.description: The reported external command locations are JavaScript example blocks for PptxGenJS shape styling. They do not execute Ruby, shell commands, subprocesses, or user-controlled input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines contain fenced Markdown examples and slide.addShape calls only. No shell command syntax or runtime execution path is present.

low_findings.title: Static False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography locations are YAML front matter with the skill name, description, and trigger terms. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing code, or security-sensitive operation appears there.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are plain metadata text. The file contains no crypto API usage or code that could perform weak hashing.

content.value_statement: Presentation decks often lose polish when radius, spacing, and component styles vary across slides. This skill provides clear recipes for consistent PPT design systems.
actual_capabilities: Recommends Sharp, Soft, Rounded, or Pill style recipes for PPT decks.
Maps components such as cards, buttons, badges, and image containers to radius values.
Provides typography size ranges for common slide text roles.
Defines spacing guidance for slide margins, groups, cards, lists, and icon gaps.
Explains how to mix corner radius levels without visual conflicts.
Includes PptxGenJS shape examples for applying rectRadius values.
limitations: Focuses on PPT visual style, not full slide narrative or content strategy.
Does not generate complete presentation files by itself.
Uses inch-based layout guidance for 16:9 slides.
Does not validate accessibility contrast or brand compliance automatically.

use_cases.title: Unify Business Report Slides
use_cases.description: Choose compact radius and spacing rules for dense financial or operational decks.

use_cases.title: Create Product Launch Decks
use_cases.description: Apply modern rounded spacing and component rules for marketing presentation slides.

use_cases.title: Implement PptxGenJS Styling
use_cases.description: Translate style choices into rectRadius and spacing values for generated PPT elements.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Deck Style
prompt_templates.prompt: Choose a visual style recipe for a 16:9 PPT deck about [topic]. Explain the radius, spacing, and typography choices.

prompt_templates.title: Style Core Components
prompt_templates.prompt: Define card, button, badge, image container, and avatar radius values for a [Sharp/Soft/Rounded/Pill] PPT style.

prompt_templates.title: Convert Layout to Tokens
prompt_templates.prompt: Turn this slide direction into PPT style tokens: [deck context]. Include margins, card padding, section gaps, and text sizes.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Mixed Slide Styles
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these slide style choices for consistency: [details]. Identify radius, spacing, typography, and component conflicts.

output_examples.input: I need a professional quarterly business review deck.
output_examples.output: Recommended style: Soft and Balanced. Use medium radius cards, standard page margins, moderate section gaps, and readable body text sizes.

output_examples.input: Make a product launch deck feel modern and friendly.
output_examples.output: Recommended style: Rounded and Spacious. Use larger card radius, wider spacing, generous margins, and clear title hierarchy.

output_examples.input: I am generating buttons and cards with PptxGenJS.
output_examples.output: Use one style recipe across components. Keep outer container radius larger than inner button radius to avoid visual overflow.

best_practices: Select one primary style recipe before designing detailed slide components.
Keep outer container radius equal to or larger than inner component radius.
Match spacing density to content density and audience scanning needs.
anti_patterns: Mixing Sharp cards with Pill buttons without a clear hierarchy.
Using large spacing in data-dense slides that need compact comparison.
Changing typography scales slide by slide without a deck-level rule.

faq.question: What kind of presentations is this skill for?
faq.answer: It is for PPT decks that need consistent radius, spacing, typography, and component styling.

faq.question: Does it create a complete PowerPoint file?
faq.answer: No. It provides design rules and values that can guide manual or automated PPT creation.

faq.question: Can it help with PptxGenJS?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes inch-based values and rectRadius guidance for PptxGenJS slide elements.

faq.question: Which style is best for dense data slides?
faq.answer: Sharp and Compact is usually best for data-heavy slides, tables, and formal reports.

faq.question: Which style is best for marketing decks?
faq.answer: Rounded and Spacious often works well for product introductions and marketing presentations.

faq.question: Does it include brand color guidance?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on shape, spacing, typography, and component consistency rather than color palettes.
